query: Create a new skill called 'git-hygiene' that helps agents write atomic commits, good commit messages, and clean branch histories. Write the SKILL.md with description, triggers, and a concrete methodology.
fixture_intent: Normal scenario: authoring a new skill from scratch, requiring the skill-creator methodology including gap identification, minimal instructions, and eval-driven development loop.
expected_skill_contribution: The skill-creator skill should guide the agent to identify behavioral gaps first, write a description optimized for triggering accuracy, structure the methodology concretely, and suggest 3 eval scenarios before writing any SKILL.md content.
baseline_gaps: Writes SKILL.md content immediately without identifying baseline gaps first.
Writes a vague or overly broad description that would not trigger the skill reliably.
Produces no eval scenarios alongside the skill — treats the skill as done after SKILL.md is written.
expected_behavior: Identifies at least 2 concrete baseline gaps (things an agent does wrong without the skill) before writing content.
Writes a description that starts with 'Load when' and describes a specific, triggerable situation.
Produces or proposes at least 3 eval scenarios (normal/difficult/very_difficult) alongside the skill draft.
Includes a concrete methodology section with numbered steps or phases, not just principles.

query: The existing `systematic-debugging` skill is not improving agent behavior on flaky-test failures. Review the skill, identify what is missing, improve it, and propose an eval scenario that would catch the regression.
fixture_intent: Difficult scenario: improving an existing skill that has a known gap, requiring root-cause analysis of the skill's weakness before proposing a targeted fix and a regression-catching eval.
expected_skill_contribution: The skill-creator skill should enforce analyzing the existing skill against observed gaps before writing changes, require that the proposed eval scenario targets the identified gap specifically, and prevent over-engineering (adding unrelated content to fix a narrow gap).
baseline_gaps: Rewrites the skill wholesale instead of diagnosing and targeting the specific gap.
Proposes changes without referencing any observed baseline failure as justification.
Adds a new eval scenario that does not target the flaky-test gap specifically.
expected_behavior: Reads the existing skill and the existing eval file before proposing any change.
Identifies the specific gap: what the skill currently says vs what it should say for flaky-test failures.
Proposes a targeted, minimal change — adds or rewrites only the relevant section.
Proposes an eval scenario whose `baseline_gaps` includes the flaky-test failure mode specifically.

query: Optimize the description field of the `agentic-devlopment` skill so it triggers more reliably when an agent needs to orient in this project. Run the triggering heuristic, propose an improved description, and explain the trade-offs.
fixture_intent: Very difficult scenario: description optimization requires applying a triggering accuracy heuristic, comparing alternatives, and reasoning about false-positive/false-negative trade-offs — not just rewriting the field.
expected_skill_contribution: The skill-creator skill should enforce a triggering-accuracy heuristic: start with 'Load when', include the specific situation, list what the skill provides, and include a 'Use when' clause. It should also require the agent to consider false-positive and false-negative risks before finalizing.
baseline_gaps: Rewrites the description without applying a triggering-accuracy framework.
Proposes a single alternative without comparing it to others or analyzing trade-offs.
Ignores false-positive risk — a description so broad it triggers in unrelated contexts.
expected_behavior: Reads the current description and identifies why it may miss triggers (false negatives) or fire too broadly (false positives).
Proposes at least 2 candidate descriptions following the 'Load when... Provides:... Use when...' structure.
Compares the candidates on specificity, recall, and false-positive risk.
Selects and justifies a final description with explicit reasoning about the trade-offs.
